original_abstract:
The ortho-substituted phenyl ring is a ubiquitous structural motif in medicinal and agricultural chemistry, appearing in over three hundred commercialized drugs and pesticides. While recent efforts to improve the physicochemical profiles of bioactive molecules have focused on replacing aromatic rings with saturated bioisosteres—a strategy often termed "escaping from flatland"—most progress has been limited to para-substituted patterns. In this study, we present 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes as a novel class of saturated bioisosteres specifically designed to mimic ortho-substituted phenyl rings. 

We developed an efficient, scalable synthetic route to these bicyclic scaffolds utilizing a triplet-sensitized [2+2] photocyclization of readily available dienes. Crystallographic analysis and exit vector plots confirm that 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes closely replicate the geometric orientations and bond angles of the ortho-phenyl system while introducing a three-dimensional character. To validate their utility, we incorporated these fragments into several bioactive compounds, including the fungicides fluxapyroxad and boscalid. The resulting saturated analogues exhibited significantly enhanced aqueous solubility (up to ten-fold) and reduced lipophilicity while crucially maintaining the biological potency of the parent molecules. This work establishes 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes as robust, chemically stable, and property-enhancing alternatives for phenyl rings in drug discovery and agrochemistry.
humanized_abstract:
Ortho-substituted phenyl rings are a very common substructure in medicinal and agricultural chemistry, being present in more than 300 marketed drugs and pesticides. Recently there has been an increasing interest in modifying bioactive molecules toward better physicochemical properties, generally implying the replacement of flat aromatic rings with more voluminous and rigid, yet still saturated, bioisosteres, a strategy named “escaping from flatland”. Even though significant advancements have been recently achieved within para-substituted patterns, much less is known about similar options for other relevant aromatic substitution patterns such as ortho. In this work, we disclose 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes as a new class of potential saturated bioisosteres specifically meant to represent ortho-substituted phenyl rings.

We have established an economic and scalable route for the synthesis of these cores by means of a triplet sensitized [2+2] photocyclisation of commercially available dienes. The structures and relative orientation of the molecules are validated by Xray crystallography and exit vector plots (EVPs), which show that 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes exhibit a similar geometry and bond vector angles as compared to the ortho-phenyl moiety, but with the added advantage of a 3D structure. These bicyclic motifs were integrated into well known bioactive compounds such as the fungicides fluxapyroxad and boscalid. Their respective saturated analogues (T1/T2) showed up to ten-fold increase in aqueous solubility, reduction of lipophilicity whilst retaining the same bioactivity as compared to their corresponding aromatic counterpart. 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes therefore offer the possibility of using robust, stable core-motifs instead of aromatic rings in Lead optimisation programs of drugs and agrochemicals.

We have established an economic and scalable route for the synthesis of these cores by means of a triplet sensitized [2+2] photocyclisation of commercially available dienes. document.input:
The ortho-substituted phenyl ring is a ubiquitous structural motif in medicinal and agricultural chemistry, appearing in over three hundred commercialized drugs and pesticides. While recent efforts to improve the physicochemical profiles of bioactive molecules have focused on replacing aromatic rings with saturated bioisosteres—a strategy often termed "escaping from flatland"—most progress has been limited to para-substituted patterns.
